biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells across the surface of the central pot, serving as a calcium amendment or physical barrier.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris outside of the pots.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Initial state shows a single, struggling green stem in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-3: The green stem shows signs of chlorosis and progressive wilting/collapse.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Total loss of the remaining stem; the biome has transitioned to a state of complete dormancy or death.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total systemic collapse. The lack of photosynthetic tissue across all pots indicates that the specimens have either died due to environmental stress (likely light deficiency or root rot) or have been removed.
anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the central pot (New Intervention).
Complete absence of expected flora (Systemic Loss).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, a single, fragile stem was visible. Over the course of the 5-day observation window, this specimen underwent rapid senescence, leading to its total disappearance. The current state is characterized by empty, dark soil in all pots, with only the white rabbit figurine remaining as a static anchor. The presence of eggshells suggests a failed attempt at soil enrichment or pest management.
